  filing_date=2023-08-04 section=business period_end=2023-06-30
Item 1. Business.
The Procter & Gamble Company (the Company) is focused on providing branded products of superior quality and value to improve the lives of the world's consumers, now and for generations to come. The Company was incorporated in Ohio in 1905, having first been established as a New Jersey corporation in 1890, and was built from a business founded in Cincinnati in 1837 by William Procter and James Gamble.
Additional information required by this item is incorporated herein by reference to Management's Discussion and Analysis (MD&A); and Notes 1 and 2 to our Consolidated Financial Statements. Unless the context indicates otherwise, the terms "Company," "P&G," "we," "our" or "us" as used herein refer to The Procter & Gamble Company (the registrant) and its subsidiaries. Throughout this Form 10-K, we incorporate by reference information from other documents filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
The Company's Annual Report on Form 10-K, quarterly reports on Form 10-Q and current reports on Form 8-K, and amendments thereto, are filed electronically with the SEC. The SEC maintains an internet site that contains these reports at: www.sec.gov. Reports can also be accessed through links from our website at: www.pginvestor.com. P&G includes the website link solely as a textual reference and the information on our website is not incorporated by reference into this report.
Copies of these reports are also available, without charge, by contacting EQ Shareowner Services, 1100 Centre Pointe Curve, Suite 101, Mendota, MN 55120-4100.
Financial Information about Segments
Information about our reportable segments can be found in the MD&A and Note 2 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Narrative Description of Business
Business Model
. Our business model is built to deliver balanced top- and bottom-line growth and value creation. We rely on the continued growth and success of existing brands and products, as well as the creation of new innovative products and brands. We offer products in markets and industry segments that ar
…[truncated 3505 chars]…
ten holding a leadership or significant market share position. We support our products with advertising, promotions and other marketing vehicles to build awareness and trial of our brands and products in conjunction with our sales force. We believe this combination provides the
2        The Procter & Gamble Company
most efficient method of marketing for these types of products. Product quality, performance, value and packaging are also important differentiating factors.
Government Regulation
. Our Company is subject to a wide variety of laws and regulations across the countries in which we do business. In the United States, many of our products and manufacturing operations are subject to one or more federal or state regulatory agencies, including the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC). We are also subject to anti-corruption laws and regulations, such as the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, and antitrust and competition laws and regulations that govern our dealings with suppliers, customers, competitors and government officials.
In addition, many foreign jurisdictions in which we do business have regulations and regulatory bodies that govern similar aspects of our operations and products, in some cases to an even more significant degree. We are also subject to expanding laws and regulations related to environmental protection and other sustainability-related matters, non-financial reporting and diligence, labor and employment, trade, taxation and data privacy and protection, including the European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and similar regulations in states within the United States and in countries around the world.
The Company has in place compliance programs and internal and external experts to help guide our business in complying with these and other existing laws and regulations that apply to us around the globe; and we have made, and plan to continue making, necessary expenditures for compliance with these laws and regulations. We also expect that our many suppliers, consultants and other third parties working on our behalf share our commitment to compliance, and we have policies and procedures in place to manage these relationships, though they inherently involve a lesser degree of control over operations and governance. We do not expect that the Company's expenditures for compliance with current government regulations, including current environmental regulations, will have a material effect on our total capital expenditures, earnings or competitive position in fiscal year 2024 as compared to prior periods.
Human Capital
. Our employees are a key source of competitive advantage. Their actions, guided by our Purpose, Values and Principles (PVPs), are critical to the long-term success of our business. We aim to retain our talented employees by offering competitive compensation and benefits, strong career development and a respectful and inclusive culture that provides equal opportunity for all.
Our Board of Directors, through the Compensation and Leadership Development Committee (C&LD Committee), provides oversight of the Company's policies and strategy relating to talent including diversity, equality and inclusion as well as the Company's compensation principles and practices. The C&LD Committee also evaluates and approves the Company's compensation plans, policies and programs applicable to our senior executives.
Employees
As of June 30, 2023, the Company had approximately 107,000 employees, an increase of 1% versus the prior year due primarily to business growth. The total number of employees is an estimate of total Company employees excluding interns, co-ops, contractors and employees of joint ventures. 49% of our employees are in manufacturing roles and 27% of our employees are located in the United States. 41% of our global employees are women and 30% of our U.S. employees identify as multicultural.
Training and Development
We focus on attracting, developing and retaining skilled and diverse talent, both from universities and the broader market. We recruit from among the best universities across markets in which we compete and are generally able to select from the top talent. We focus on developing our employees by providing a variety of job experiences, training programs and skill development opportunities. Given our develop-from-within model for staffing most of our senior leadership positions, it is particularly important for us to ensure holistic growth and full engagement of our employees.
Diversity, Equality and Inclusion
As a consumer products company, we believe that it is important for our workforce to reflect the diversity of our consumers worldwide. We also seek to foster an inclusive work environment where each individual can bring their authentic self, which helps drive innovation and enables us to better serve our consumers. We aspire to achieve equal gender representation globally and at key management and leadership levels. Within the U.S. workforce, our aspiration is to achieve 40% multicultural representation overall as well as at management and leadership levels.
Compensation and Benefits
Market-competitive compensation and reward programs are critical elements of our employee value equation to attract and retain the best talent. Our total rewards programs are based on the principles of paying for performance, paying competitively versus peer companies that we compete with for talent in the marketplace and focusing on long-term success through a combination of short-term and long-term incentive programs. We also offer competitive benefit programs, including retirement plans and health insurance in line with local country practices with flexibility to accommodate the needs of a diverse workforce.
Sustainability
. Environmental sustainability is integrated into our business strategy to offer consumers irresistibly superior products that are more sustainable. Our aim is to deliver balanced top- and bottom-line growth, value creation and key sustainability objectives. In 2021, the Company announced a 2040 net zero ambition and published a Climate Transition Action
The Procter & Gamble Company        3
Plan, which describes the Company's ongoing efforts toward reducing greenhouse gas emissions across scopes 1 and 2 and elements of scope 3. This includes a long-term objective of net zero emissions for scopes 1 and 2, elements of scope 3 and interim goals to help us pace our progress. The Company has also declared goals towards using renewable electricity for our operations, reducing use of virgin petroleum-based plastic in packaging, increasing the recyclability or reusability of packaging, responsible sourcing of key forest-based commodities, improving efficiency of water usage in our operations and driving a global portfolio of water restoration projects to address water scarcity.
We use the standards and guidelines of the Global Reporting Initiative, Sustainability Accounting Standards Board (SASB) industry specific standards and the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures (TCFD) to inform our sustainability and related disclosures included in this Annual Report, our Proxy Statement and our sustainability reports. The "materiality" thresholds in those standards and guidelines may differ from the concept of "materiality" for purposes of the federal securities laws and disclosures required by the Commission's rules in this Annual Report. References to our sustainability reports and website are for informational purposes only and neither the sustainability reports nor the other information on our website is incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K. Additional detailed information on our sustainability efforts can be found on our website at https://pginvestor.com/esg.
PG.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: Procter & Gamble (PG)**
**Assessment Date: 2024-01-01 | Evidence Cutoff: 2023-12-29**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
*   **Revenue & Earnings Growth:** Latest quarter (Q1 2023-09-30) revenue was $21.87B, representing QoQ growth of +6.4% and YoY growth of +6.1%. Diluted EPS was $1.83, showing strong QoQ growth of +33.6% and YoY growth of +16.6%. TTM revenue was $83.27B.
*   **Margins:** Gross margin (latest Q1) was 52.0% ($11.37B / $21.87B). Operating margin was 26.4% ($5.77B / $21.87B). Net margin (from valuation data) was 18.4%.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total equity grew from $44.89B (2021-12-31) to $48.01B (2023-09-30). Total liabilities were $74.52B. The debt-to-equity ratio was 1.55x ($74.52B / $48.01B).
*   **Cash Flow:** Data unavailable for the entire 8-quarter period.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** Net margin has been stable, ranging from 17.8% to 18.4% over the last four reported quarters. Return on equity (ROE) was 31.9% (latest). Without cash flow statements, the cash conversion of these earnings cannot be quantitatively assessed.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Total equity has demonstrated a stable, positive trajectory. The leverage ratio (Liabilities/Equity) was 1.55x as of 2023-09-30.

**3. Valuation vs. Company's Own History**
*   **P/E Diluted:** The latest P/E was 23.66x (2023-12-29). This is at the lower end of its 365-day range of 23.2x to 27.0x. The historical 12-point series shows the P/E declining from 25.94x in early January 2023.
*   **P/B:** The latest price-to-book was 7.55x, within its 365-day range of 7.4x to 8.7x.
*   **Earnings Yield:** The latest earnings yield was 4.2%.

**4. Price Level and Returns vs. Fundamental Path**
*   **Price Level:** The stock closed at $146.54 on 2023-12-29, within its 52-week range of $135.83 to $158.38.
*   **Returns:** The quarterly return (from 2023-09-29) was +0.5%. The annual return (from 2022-12-28) was -3.6%.
*   **Context:** The flat-to-negative price returns occurred alongside reported fundamental improvements: QoQ and YoY revenue and EPS growth, and expanding margins in the latest quarter. The valuation (P/E) has compressed over the year despite these fundamental gains.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
*   **Upside:** The strongest quantitative signal is the combination of positive fundamental momentum (Q1 2023: revenue +6.1% YoY, EPS +16.6% YoY) with valuation compression (P/E of 23.66x at the lower end of its recent range). This presents a potential mean-reversion scenario if growth persists.
*   **Downside:** The absence of free cash flow data is a material gap in assessing true economic earnings. The leverage ratio of 1.55x (Debt/Equity) and the lack of cash flow metrics limit the assessment of balance-sheet resilience and capital return capacity. The stock's annual price return of -3.6% indicates negative momentum that may not have concluded.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
Based strictly on the supplied point-in-time numbers: The fundamental trajectory shows accelerating revenue and earnings growth with stable margins. Valuation, measured by P/E, has contracted to the lower end of its recent range despite this positive trajectory. The primary quantitative risk is the lack of cash flow data, preventing confirmation of earnings quality and cash generation. The primary quantitative reward is the disconnect between improving reported fundamentals and a lower valuation multiple. The risk/reward profile appears neutral to slightly positive contingent on the durability of the earnings growth, but is materially incomplete without cash flow evidence.
PG.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Equity Research Report: Procter & Gamble (PG)**
**Date of Assessment:** 2024-01-01
**Evidence Cutoff:** 2023-12-31

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Based on the 2023-08-04 10-K filing, Procter & Gamble operates a resilient, globally diversified consumer staples franchise. The company focuses on branded products of superior quality and value, sold in approximately 180 countries. Its business model is built on balanced top- and bottom-line growth, relying on innovation and the creation of new products and brands. The competitive strategy centers on delivering meaningful superiority across five vectors: product performance, packaging, brand communication, retail execution, and consumer value. P&G holds leadership or significant market share in the highly competitive segments in which it operates. Key operational strengths include a global supply chain and a "develop-from-within" talent model. The company has a stated commitment to environmental sustainability, including a net-zero emissions ambition for 2040.

### 2. Recent Narrative Developments (Dated)
*   **Growth & Outlook:** As noted on **2023-12-18**, analyst consensus estimates forecast revenue of $21.73 billion for the upcoming quarter, representing 4.63% growth year-over-year, and full-year earnings of $6.42 per share.
*   **Operational Improvement:** Reports from **2023-12-08** highlight P&G's "supply chain 3.0" program, initiated in the prior fiscal year, which is driving improved capacity, agility, flexibility, and resilience.
*   **Market Perception:** Throughout December 2023 (e.g., **2023-12-21**, **2023-12-22**), multiple financial blogs and analyst pieces frequently cited PG as a reliable dividend stock and a core holding for stability and 2024 portfolio construction, reflecting its defensive characteristics.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
*   **Litigation & Regulatory Risk:** The company faces multiple active lawsuits related to product safety and marketing.
    *   A proposed class action (**2023-12-21**) alleges high lead levels in Metamucil.
    *   An $8M class action settlement was reached (**2023-12-12**) over benzene in aerosol products.
    *   The company was penalized for misleading packaging (**2023-12-07**).
    *   A patent infringement lawsuit seeks to block a competitor's diaper products (**2023-12-31**).
*   **Offset:** The 10-K (**2023-08-04**) acknowledges a broad, global regulatory landscape but states that compliance expenditures for existing regulations are not expected to materially impact FY2024 results compared to prior periods. The company maintains compliance programs to manage these risks.

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral.** The evidence portrays a strong, well-managed franchise with a durable business model, operational improvement initiatives, and consistent market recognition as a defensive asset. This is balanced against a series of contemporaneous legal and regulatory challenges concerning product safety and labeling, which represent tangible reputational and financial risks, though the company does not expect them to be material to its near-term financial performance relative to its scale. The assessment is Neutral, reflecting a stable core business offset by the aggregate weight of ongoing litigation.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Net margin (from valuation data) was 18.4%' for the latest quarter, but the raw income statement shows net_income=4.56B / revenue=21.87B = 20.9%, not 18.4%. The 18.4% figure comes from the valuation ratios snapshot (trailing basis), not the latest quarter's income statement. This is a misattribution that could mislead on the margin trajectory.
corrections.required_action: Clarify that 18.4% is the trailing net margin from the valuation data point, not the latest quarter's computed margin. The latest quarter's net margin is ~20.9%.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Gross margin (latest Q1) was 52.0% ($11.37B / $21.87B)' and 'Operating margin was 26.4% ($5.77B / $21.87B)'. The raw valuation data shows gross_margin=0.491 and operating_margin=0.228. The 52.0% and 26.4% figures are correct per the income statement for Q1 2023-09-30, but the valuation data's gross_margin and operating_margin appear to be trailing figures. The report does not distinguish between quarterly and trailing margins, which could create confusion.
corrections.required_action: Note that the 52.0% gross margin and 26.4% operating margin are Q1-specific, while the valuation data's 49.1% and 22.8% are trailing figures. The report should clarify this distinction.

corrections.issue: Qual report states 'an $8M class action settlement was reached (2023-12-12) over benzene in aerosol products'. The raw evidence confirms this. However, the qual report also states 'A patent infringement lawsuit seeks to block a competitor's diaper products (2023-12-31)'. The raw evidence for 2023-12-31 describes P&G seeking to block Kimberly-Clark's Huggies — this is P&G as plaintiff, not defendant. Framing it as a risk without noting P&G initiated the suit is misleading.
corrections.required_action: Clarify that the 2023-12-31 diaper lawsuit item is P&G as plaintiff seeking to block a competitor, not P&G as defendant. This is an offensive IP action, not a defensive risk.

unsupported_claims: : Quant report claims 'Net margin (from valuation data) was 18.4%' in the context of Q1 margins. The raw income statement yields 20.9% (4.56B/21.87B). The 18.4% is a trailing valuation-data figure, not the quarterly margin.

: Qual report lists 'A patent infringement lawsuit seeks to block a competitor's diaper products (2023-12-31)' as a material qualitative risk. The raw evidence shows P&G is the plaintiff seeking to block Kimberly-Clark's Huggies, making this an offensive action, not a risk to P&G.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: PG presents a classic defensive franchise with improving fundamentals offset by elevated valuation and legal overhang. The disconnect between accelerating earnings growth (+16.6% YoY) and P/E compression to the lower end of its range (23.66x) offers modest upside, but the lack of cash flow data and ongoing litigation temper conviction.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: PG presents a classic defensive franchise with improving fundamentals offset by elevated valuation and legal overhang. The disconnect between accelerating earnings growth (+16.6% YoY) and P/E compression to the lower end of its range (23.66x) offers modest upside, but the lack of cash flow data and ongoing litigation temper conviction.
key_factors: Q1 2023-09-30 showed accelerating revenue growth of +6.1% YoY and EPS growth of +16.6% YoY, with operating margin of 26.4%, demonstrating continued pricing power and operational execution.

P/E of 23.66x as of 2023-12-29 sits at the lower end of its 365-day range (23.2x-27.0x), representing valuation compression despite positive fundamental momentum, creating a potential mean-reversion opportunity.

Multiple active lawsuits including benzene settlement ($8M, 2023-12-12) and Metamucil lead allegations (2023-12-21) represent ongoing reputational and financial risk, though the company does not expect material FY2024 impact.
Debt-to-equity of 1.55x and absence of free cash flow data limit assessment of true economic earnings and balance-sheet resilience, creating uncertainty about capital return capacity.

Latest 10-K business section (newest first):
  filing_date=2023-01-27 section=business period_end=2022-12-31
Item 1. Business
The Boeing Company, together with its subsidiaries (herein referred to as "Boeing," the "Company," "we," "us," "our"), is one of the world's major aerospace firms.
We are organized based on the products and services we offer. We operate in four reportable segments:
- 
Commercial Airplanes (BCA);
- 
Defense, Space & Security (BDS);
- 
Global Services (BGS);
- 
Boeing Capital (BCC).
Commercial Airplanes Segment
This segment develops, produces and markets commercial jet aircraft principally to the commercial airline industry worldwide. We are a leading producer of commercial aircraft and offer a family of commercial jetliners designed to meet a broad spectrum of global passenger and cargo requirements of airlines. This family of commercial jet aircraft in production includes the 737 narrow-body model and the 767, 777 and 787 wide-body models. We ended production of the 747 wide-body model in 2022. Development continues on the 777X program and the 737-7 and 737-10 derivatives.
Defense, Space & Security Segment
This segment engages in the research, development, production and modification of manned and unmanned military aircraft and weapons systems for strike, surveillance and mobility, including fighter and trainer aircraft; vertical lift, including rotorcraft and tilt-rotor aircraft; and commercial derivative aircraft, including anti-submarine and tanker aircraft. In addition, this segment engages in the research, development, production and modification of the following products and related services: strategic defense and intelligence systems, including strategic missile and defense systems, command, control, communications, computers, intelligence, surveillance and reconnaissance (C4ISR), cyber and information solutions, and intelligence systems, satellite systems, including government and commercial satellites and space exploration.
Global Services Segment
This segment provides services to our commercial and defense customers worldwide. BGS sustains aerospace platforms and systems with a full spectrum of products and services, including supply chain and logistics management, engineering, maintenance and modifications, upgrades and conversions, spare parts, pilot and maintenance training systems and services, technical and maintenance documents, and data analytics and digital services.
Boeing Capital Segment
BCC seeks to ensure that Boeing customers have the financing they need to buy and take delivery of their Boeing product, while managing overall financing exposure. BCC's portfolio consists of equipment under operating leases, sal
…[truncated 7741 chars]…
ion (FAA) and the Department of Homeland Security. Similar government authorities exist in our non-U.S. markets.
Government Contracts.
The U.S. government, and other governments, may terminate any of our government contracts at their convenience, as well as for default based on our failure to meet specified performance requirements. If any of our U.S. government contracts were to be terminated for convenience, we generally would be entitled to receive payment for work completed and allowable termination or cancellation costs. If any of our government contracts were to be terminated for default, generally the U.S. government would pay only for the work that has been accepted and could require us to pay the difference between the original contract price and the cost to re-procure the contract items, net of the work accepted from the original contract. The U.S. government can also hold us liable for damages resulting from the default.
Commercial Aircraft.
In the U.S., our commercial aircraft products are required to comply with FAA regulations governing production and quality systems, airworthiness and installation approvals, repair procedures and continuing operational safety. New aircraft models and new derivative aircraft are required to obtain FAA certification prior to entry into service. Outside the U.S., similar requirements exist for airworthiness, installation and operational approvals. These requirements are generally administered by the national aviation authorities of each country and, in the case of Europe, coordinated by the European Union Aviation Safety Agency.
Environmental.
We are subject to various federal, state, local and non-U.S. laws and regulations relating to environmental protection, including the discharge, treatment, storage, disposal and remediation of hazardous substances and wastes. We could also be affected by laws and regulations relating to climate change, including laws limiting or otherwise related to greenhouse gas emissions. These laws and regulations could lead to increased environmental compliance expenditures, increased energy and raw materials costs and new and/or additional investment in designs and technologies. We continually assess our compliance status and management of environmental matters to ensure our operations are in compliance with all applicable environmental laws and regulations. Investigation, remediation and operation and maintenance costs associated with environmental compliance and management of sites are a normal, recurring part of our operations. These costs often are allowable costs under our contracts with the U.S. government. It is reasonably possible that costs incurred to ensure continued environmental compliance could have a material impact on our results of operations, financial condition or cash flows if additional work requirements or more stringent clean-up standards are imposed by regulators, new areas of soil, air and groundwater contamination are discovered and/or expansions of work scope are prompted by the results of investigations.
For additional information relating to environmental contingencies, see Note 13 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Non-U.S. Sales.
Our non-U.S. sales are subject to both U.S. and non-U.S. governmental regulations and procurement policies and practices, including regulations relating to import-export control, tariffs, investment, exchange controls, anti-corruption and repatriation of earnings. Non-U.S. sales are also subject to varying currency, political and economic risks.
Raw Materials, Parts and Subassemblies
We are highly dependent on the availability of essential materials, parts and subassemblies from our suppliers and subcontractors. The most important raw materials required for our aerospace products
4
Table of Contents
are aluminum (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions), titanium (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions) and composites (including carbon and boron). Although alternative sources generally exist for these raw materials, qualification of the sources could take a year or more. During 2022, as a result of the Russia Ukraine war, we suspended purchasing titanium from Russia. This has not disrupted our operations as we have been able to use inventory on hand and identify alternative sources. Many major components and product equipment items are procured or subcontracted on a sole-source basis. We continue to work with a small number of sole-source suppliers to ensure continuity of supply for certain items.
Suppliers
We are dependent upon the ability of a large number of U.S. and non-U.S. suppliers and subcontractors to meet performance specifications, quality standards and delivery schedules at our anticipated costs. While we maintain an extensive qualification and performance surveillance system to control risk associated with such reliance on third parties, failure of suppliers or subcontractors to meet commitments could adversely affect production schedules and program/contract profitability, thereby jeopardizing our ability to fulfill commitments to our customers. We are also dependent on the availability of energy sources, such as electricity, at affordable prices.
Seasonality
No material portion of our business is considered to be seasonal.
Executive Officers of the Registrant
See "Item 10. Directors, Executive Officers and Corporate Governance" in Part III.
Other Information
Boeing was originally incorporated in the State of Washington in 1916 and reincorporated in Delaware in 1934. Our principal executive offices are located at 929 Long Bridge Drive, Arlington, Virginia 22202, and our telephone number is (703) 465-3500.
General information about us can be found at www.boeing.com. The information contained on or connected to our website is not incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K and should not be considered part of this or any other report filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Our Annual Report on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q and Current Reports on Form 8-K, as well as any amendments to those reports, are available free of charge through our website as soon as reasonably practicable after we file them with, or furnish them to, the SEC. The SEC maintains a website at www.sec.gov that contains reports, proxy statements and other information regarding SEC registrants, including Boeing.
Forward-Looking Statements
This report, as well as our annual report to shareholders, quarterly reports and other filings we make with the SEC, press and earnings releases and other written and oral communications, contain "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Words such as "may," "should," "expects," "intends," "projects," "plans," "believes," "estimates," "targets," "anticipates" and similar expressions generally identify these forward-looking statements. Examples of forward-looking statements include statements relating to our future financial condition and operating results, as well as any other statement that does not directly relate to any historical or current fact.

Forward-looking statements are based on expectations and assumptions that we believe to be reasonable when made, but that may not prove to be accurate. These statements are not guarantees and are subject to risks, uncertainties and changes in circumstances that are difficult to predict. Many factors, including those set forth in the "Risk Factors" section below and other important factors disclosed in this report and from time to time in our other filings with the SEC, could cause actual results to differ materially and adversely from these forward-looking statements. Any forward-looking statement speaks only as of the date on which it is made, and we assume no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statement whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise, except as required by law.
BA.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: Boeing (BA)**
**Date: 2024-01-01 (Evidence PIT)**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
*   **Revenue:** TTM revenue is $75.76B. Quarterly revenue shows volatility, with the latest Q3 2023 at $18.10B, representing a QoQ decline of -8.3% but a YoY increase of +13.5%.
*   **Earnings & Margins:** TTM net income is -$2.88B. The latest quarterly EPS is -$2.70. Profitability is negative; the latest reported gross margin is 9.1%, operating margin is -1.9%, and net margin is -3.8%. Net margin has improved from -13.9% in early 2023 to -3.8% at year-end.
*   **Cash Flow:** Cash flow data is unavailable for the provided periods.
*   **Balance Sheet:** As of 2023-09-30, total equity is -$16.72B. Total debt is $52.03B. The debt-to-asset ratio is 0.39 ($52.03B / $134.28B). Equity has been consistently negative over the entire provided period (8 quarters).

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** Persistently negative net income and negative equity suggest poor earnings quality. The company is not generating net profit from operations. The YoY EPS change of -50.8% in the latest quarter underscores continued fundamental pressure.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Negative shareholders' equity of -$16.72B is a significant quantitative marker of insolvency risk. High financial leverage is evident with $52.03B in debt against negative equity. Resilience is structurally weak based on these figures. Cash position data is unavailable.

**3. Valuation vs. Supplied History**
*   The latest price-to-sales (P/S) ratio is 2.088. Valuation ratios dependent on positive earnings (P/E, EV/EBIT) are not applicable (n/a) due to negative earnings.
*   The net margin trajectory, the only supplied valuation context, shows improvement from -0.139 to -0.038 over the 2023 window, but remains negative. Valuation cannot be assessed as cheap via traditional earnings multiples given the negative earnings profile.

**4. Price Level & Return Context**
*   **Price:** The latest close (2023-12-29) is $260.66, near the 52-week high of $267.54.
*   **Returns:** The stock has experienced a strong quarterly price increase of +36.0% and an annual increase of +38.4%. The quarterly path shows a sharp reversal, with a -9.2% decline in Q3 2023 followed by a +36.0% rebound in Q4 2023.
*   **Fundamental Context:** The price appreciation has occurred against a backdrop of persistent negative earnings, negative equity, and high leverage. There is no supplied fundamental trajectory (e.g., revenue growth, margin expansion) that quantitatively explains the magnitude of the recent price move.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** The strongest quantitative upside signal is the +13.5% YoY revenue growth in the latest quarter, indicating potential commercial momentum. The improvement in net margin from -13.9% to -3.8% is a positive trajectory.
*   **Downside:** The strongest quantitative downside risk is the profound balance sheet weakness: negative equity of -$16.72B coupled with $52.03B in debt. This represents high financial risk. Another downside is the latest quarter's sharp QoQ EPS deterioration from -$0.25 to -$2.70.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
Based strictly on the supplied numbers, the risk/reward profile appears unfavorable. The reward is predicated on revenue growth and margin improvement trends. However, the balance sheet presents severe quantitative risks: negative equity and high debt load indicate financial fragility. The stock's valuation (P/S of 2.088) is not supported by positive earnings or strong cash flow (unavailable), and the recent price surge (+38.4% annually) is disconnected from the fundamental picture of persistent losses. The quantitative evidence suggests high downside financial risk is present.
BA.qualitative_report:
**Boeing (BA) Qualitative Assessment – Point-in-Time to 2024-01-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Boeing is a major aerospace firm organized into four segments: Commercial Airplanes (BCA), Defense, Space & Security (BDS), Global Services (BGS), and Boeing Capital (BCC). BCA develops and produces commercial jets (737, 777, 787 families) in a duopolistic market with Airbus. BDS engages in military aircraft, weapons, and space systems, facing strong competition from Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, and SpaceX. BGS provides a full spectrum of aftermarket services. The company is heavily dependent on suppliers for essential materials and operates under stringent FAA and other global regulatory oversight.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Safety/Quality:** On 2023-12-28, Boeing urged inspections of 737 Max jets for possible loose bolts, indicating ongoing operational focus on safety and quality control.
*   **Commercial Orders/Deliveries:** On 2023-12-27, Boeing was set to deliver five 787 Dreamliners to LATAM Group, signaling an improved customer relationship and potential for expanded presence in Latin America.
*   **Competition/Pricing:** As of 2023-12-30, Boeing and Lockheed were noted as having matched SpaceX's launch prices in their space launch subsidiary, which could imply competitive pressure but also potential for improved profitability.
*   **Analyst Sentiment:** An analyst noted on 2023-11-28 that Boeing boasts a "favorable 2024 outlook," with sustainable growth in commercial and defense segments. RBC upgraded the stock on 2023-11-29, citing "improving fundamentals."
*   **Market Outlook:** On 2023-12-26, Airbus projected a need for double the number of passenger and freighter aircraft by 2041, underscoring the long-term demand backdrop for the industry.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Recurring Quality/Safety Issues:** The need for inspections on 737 Max planes (2023-12-28) continues a narrative of production and quality challenges. Historical documents show Boeing paid a $12 million fine in 2015 to settle FAA safety investigations (2023-12-22), and allegations about internal warnings on toxic risks (2023-12-25) pose reputational and potential legal risks.
    *   **Labor Relations:** The 10-K (2023-01-27) notes key union contracts (representing ~20% and 10% of employees) expire in 2024 and 2025, presenting a near-term operational risk.
    *   **Supply Chain Dependence:** The business is highly dependent on suppliers for materials like aluminum and titanium, with sole-source arrangements for many components (10-K, 2023-01-27).
*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Duopoly Structure:** The commercial aircraft market is a duopoly with Airbus, providing a structural advantage and pricing power (10-K, 2023-01-27; web context 2023-12-09).
    *   **Diversified & Services-Oriented:** The portfolio spans commercial, defense, and a growing services segment (BGS), which provides recurring revenue.
    *   **Positive Long-Term Demand:** Industry projections for massive fleet renewal and growth (Airbus, 2023-12-26) support the fundamental demand thesis for Boeing's core products.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral/Negative.** The franchise operates in a structurally favorable duopoly with long-term demand tailwinds, and recent analyst commentary highlights an improving fundamental outlook for 2024. However, the confirmed news flow is dominated by ongoing safety and quality scrutiny (737 Max inspections) and references to historical regulatory and reputational liabilities. These operational and reputational risks, combined with impending major labor contract expirations, create significant near-term uncertainty that tempers the positive structural and cyclical factors. The balance of recent point-in-time evidence leans toward a cautious view.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Cash flow data is unavailable' and 'Cash position data is unavailable,' but the raw balance sheet pack contains cash figures for several quarters (2022-09-30: $14.30B, 2022-06-30: $11.40B, 2022-03-31: $12.30B, 2021-12-31: $16.20B). While the most recent quarters (2023) lack cash data, the report should have noted the available historical cash positions to provide context on liquidity trends rather than blanket-stating unavailability.
corrections.required_action: Acknowledge that historical cash data exists in the balance sheet pack for 2021-2022 periods and note the declining trend from $16.2B to $14.3B, while confirming 2023 cash figures are indeed absent.

corrections.issue: Quant report states YoY EPS change of '-50.8%' as evidence of 'continued fundamental pressure,' but the raw growth panel shows YoY EPS diluted = -50.8% while YoY net_income = -50.5%. However, the QoQ EPS change is listed as +980.0% (from -$2.06 to -$2.70 is actually a deterioration, not improvement). The +980% figure appears to be a raw data artifact (likely reflecting a move from a deeply negative base). The report correctly identifies the QoQ EPS deterioration from -$0.25 to -$2.70 in the downside section but does not reconcile this with the +980% QoQ figure in the growth panel, which could mislead.
corrections.required_action: Clarify that the +980% QoQ EPS change in the raw data is a mathematical artifact of moving from a large negative base and does not represent improvement; the absolute EPS worsened from -$0.25 to -$2.70.

corrections.issue: Qual report cites 'Boeing paid a $12 million fine in 2015 to settle FAA safety investigations (2023-12-22)' and 'allegations about internal warnings on toxic risks (2023-12-25)' as material risks. These are sourced from the raw evidence pack (AP News and NR Today articles). However, the qual report frames the 2015 fine as a 'historical' risk without noting that the AP News article summary indicates the documents were newly revealed despite the settlement occurring in 2015, which is relevant context for assessing ongoing reputational exposure.
corrections.required_action: Minor: the characterization is acceptable but could note that the 2015 fine documents were newly surfaced in 2023 reporting, adding to current reputational risk rather than being purely historical.

unsupported_claims: : The quant report's statement 'Cash flow data is unavailable for the provided periods' and 'Cash position data is unavailable' is partially inaccurate — the raw balance sheet pack includes cash figures for 2021-12-31 through 2022-09-30 ($16.2B, $12.3B, $11.4B, $14.3B respectively).
: The qual report's Neutral/Negative assessment is well-supported by the evidence pack, with safety/quality concerns, labor risks, and regulatory liabilities properly cited from dated sources.

: Both reports correctly identify the core tension: strong price performance (+38.4% annual) against a backdrop of persistent losses, negative equity, and high leverage. No material unsupported claims found in the directional conclusion.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Boeing's duopoly franchise and improving margin trajectory (-13.9% to -3.8% net margin) provide a structural floor, but the stock's +38.4% annual run to near 52-week highs on persistent negative equity (-$16.72B) and ongoing losses (-$2.88B TTM) leaves limited risk-adjusted upside. The risk/reward is roughly balanced at current levels.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Boeing's duopoly franchise and improving margin trajectory (-13.9% to -3.8% net margin) provide a structural floor, but the stock's +38.4% annual run to near 52-week highs on persistent negative equity (-$16.72B) and ongoing losses (-$2.88B TTM) leaves limited risk-adjusted upside. The risk/reward is roughly balanced at current levels.
key_factors: Revenue growth of +13.5% YoY in Q3 2023 and net margin improvement from -13.9% to -3.8% over 2023 signal operational recovery momentum, but profitability remains deeply negative with TTM net income of -$2.88B.

Negative shareholders' equity of -$16.72B with $52.03B in debt represents severe balance sheet fragility; historical cash declined from $16.2B (2021-12) to $14.3B (2022-09) with no 2023 data available, limiting visibility on liquidity.

Stock price of $260.66 near 52-week high of $267.54 with P/S of 2.088 and +38.4% annual return appears to already price in the turnaround narrative, leaving little margin of safety given ongoing safety/quality scrutiny (737 Max bolt inspections, 2023-12-28) and expiring union contracts in 2024-2025.

Duopoly structure with Airbus and massive long-term demand backdrop (Airbus projects double fleet by 2041) provide structural franchise value that supports a floor, but near-term catalysts are limited and risks from labor negotiations and regulatory scrutiny are elevated.

ITEM 1. Business
The Walt Disney Company, together with its subsidiaries, is a diversified worldwide entertainment company with operations in three segments: Entertainment, Sports and Experiences.
The terms "Company", "we", "our" and "us" are used in this report to refer collectively to the parent company and the subsidiaries through which businesses are conducted.
Human Capital
The Company's key human capital management objectives are to attract, retain and develop the highest quality talent. To support these objectives, the Company's human resources programs are designed to develop talent to prepare them for critical roles and leadership positions for the future; reward and support employees through competitive pay, benefit and perquisite programs; enhance the Company's culture through efforts aimed at making the workplace more engaging and inclusive; acquire talent and facilitate internal talent mobility to create a high-performing, diverse workforce; engage employees as brand ambassadors of the Company's content, products and experiences; and evolve and invest in technology, tools and resources to enable employees at work.
The Company employed approximately 225,000 people as of September 30, 20

xercised its put right to require the Company to purchase NBCU's interest in Hulu (see Note 2 of the Consolidated Financial Statements for additional information).

TABLE OF CONTENTS
Content Sales/Licensing and Other
The majority of Content Sales/Licensing revenue is derived from TV/VOD, theatrical and home entertainment distribution. In addition, revenue is generated from music distribution, stage plays and post-production services through Industrial Light & Magic and Skywalker Sound.
The Company also publishes National Geographic magazine, which is reported with Content Sales/Licensing.
TV/VOD Distribution
We license our content to third-party television networks, television stations and other video service providers for distribution to viewers on television or a variety of internet-connected devices, including through other DTC services.
Theatrical Distribution
The Company licenses full-length live-action and animated films to theaters globally. Cumulatively through September 30, 2023, the Company has released approximately 1,100 full-length live-action films and 100 full-length animated films. In the domestic and most major international markets, we generally distribute and market our films directly. In certain international markets our films are distributed by independent companies. In some territories, certain films may be exclusively distributed on our DTC streaming services. During fiscal 2024, we expect to release approximately 15 films, although the ultimate number of releases will depend on when productions resume following the writers/actors' work stoppages.
The Company incurs significant marketing and advertising costs before and throughout the theatrical release of a film in an effort to generate public awareness of the film, to increase the public's intent to view the film and to help generate consumer interest in the subsequent home entertainment and other ancillary markets. These costs are expensed as incurred, which may result in a loss on a film in the theatrical markets, including in periods prior to the theatrical release of the film.
Home Entertainment Distribution
We distribute the Company's film and episodic content in home entertainment markets on DVD and Blu-ray disc, through electronic home video licenses and VOD rentals globally.
Domestically and internationally, we distribute directly to retailers and through independent distribution companies. Electronic formats of our film and episodic content may be purchased through e-tailers such as Apple and Amazon, and MVPDs, such as Comcast and DirecTV, and physical formats are generally sold to retailers, such as Walmart and Target. The Company also operates Disney Movie Club, which sells DVD/Blu-ray discs directly to consumers in the U.S. and Canada.
Distribution of film content in the home entertainment window generally starts within three months after the theatrical release. Electronic formats are typically available approximately four to eight weeks ahead of the physical release. We also license titles to VOD e-tailers concurrent with physical home entertainment distribution.
Distribution of episodic content in the home entertainment window includes electronic sales of season passes that can be purchased prior to, during and after the broadcast season with individual episodes typically available to season pass customers shortly after the initial airing of the show in each territory. Access to individual episodes is also available for electronic purchase shortly after the initial airing in each territory.
Disney Theatrical Group
Disney Theatrical Group develops, produces and licenses live entertainment events on Broadway and around the world. Productions include The Lion King, Frozen, Aladdin and Beauty and the Beast.
Disney Theatrical Group also licenses the Company's IP to Feld Entertainment, the producer of Disney On Ice and Marvel Universe Live!.
Disney Music Group
The Disney Music Group encompasses all aspects of the Company's music commercialization and marketing including: recorded music (Walt Disney Records and Hollywood Records); music publishing; and concerts. Disney Music Group distributes music both physically and digitally and also licenses music throughout the world in various forms of media, including: television; print; gaming; and consumer products.
Equity Investment
The Company has a 30% effective interest in Tata Play Limited, which operates a direct-to-home satellite distribution platform in India.
Content Production and Acquisition
Produced content primarily consists of original films and episodic programs, network news and daytime/nighttime content and licensed content includes acquired episodic programming rights. Original content is generally produced under the following banners: ABC Signature; Disney Branded Television; FX Productions; Lucasfilm; Marvel; National Geographic Studios; Pixar;

Costs to produce content are generally capitalized and allocated across Entertainment's businesses based on the estimated relative value of the distribution windows.
Generally, the Company has full production and distribution rights to its IP. However, prior to the Company's acquisition of Marvel, Sony Pictures Entertainment licensed from Marvel the rights to produce and distribute Spider-Man films in all windows except for the merchandise rights, which the Company retains.
The Company has a significant library of content spanning approximately 100 years of production history as well as acquired libraries. In addition, the library includes approximately 100 series and 65 films that were produced for initial distribution on our DTC platforms.

Competition and Seasonality
Linear Networks and Direct-to-Consumer compete for viewers' attention and audience share primarily with other television networks, independent television stations and other media, such as other DTC streaming services, social media and video games. Our television and radio stations primarily compete for audiences and advertisers in local market areas.
Linear Networks compete with other networks for carriage by MVPDs. The Company's contractual agreements with MVPDs are renewed or renegotiated from time to time in the ordinary course of business. **Quantitative Report: DIS (as of 2024-01-01)**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
*   **Revenue:** TTM revenue is $88.90B. The latest quarter (2023-09-30) shows revenue of $21.24B, a -4.9% QoQ decline but +5.4% YoY growth. The quarterly path is volatile, ranging from $19.25B (2022-04-02) to $23.51B (2022-12-31).
*   **Earnings:** TTM net income is $3.39B. Quarterly net income and EPS are highly volatile, including a net loss of -$153.00M (EPS: -$0.25) in Q3 2023 and a net income of $1.49B (EPS: $0.69) in Q2 2023. The latest quarter (Q4 2023) net income is $694.00M (EPS: $0.15).
*   **Margins:** TTM operating income is $12.86B. Quarterly operating income ranges from $1.60B (2022-10-01) to $3.70B (2022-04-02). The latest operating margin (2023-09-30) is 14.0% ($2.98B / $21.24B). The net margin for the period is 3.3% ($694M / $21.24B).
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total equity has grown steadily from $94.31B (2022-01-01) to $103.96B (2023-09-30). Total debt was $46.43B as of 2023-09-30, down from $48.37B in 2022-10-01. The debt-to-equity ratio is 0.447.
*   **Cash Flow:** No quarterly or annual cash flow data is supplied.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** Earnings quality appears low. Quarterly net income and EPS exhibit extreme volatility, including a significant loss in Q3 2023. The YoY growth in EPS (+66.7%) and net income (+173.2%) for the latest quarter is flattered by a weak prior-year comparable (Q4 2022 EPS: $0.09). The absence of cash flow data prevents assessment of cash conversion.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The balance sheet shows resilience. Total equity has increased for 8 consecutive quarters, from $94.31B to $103.96B. The debt-to-equity ratio of 0.447 is moderate. Total liabilities have decreased from $109.00B (2022-01-01) to $101.62B (2023-09-30).

**3. Valuation versus Supplied History**
*   The latest valuation (2023-12-29) shows a P/E (diluted) of 69.99, a P/B of 1.589, and a P/S of 1.859.
*   Over the 2023 window, the P/E ranged from 33.9 to 74.5. The current P/E of 69.99 is near the top of this range.
*   The P/B of 1.589 is within its 2023 range of 1.4 to 2.1.
*   The net margin of 0.038 and ROE of 0.033 are at the lower end of their 2023 ranges (net margin: 0.0-0.1; ROE: 0.0-0.0).

**4. Price Level and Return Context versus Fundamentals**
*   **Price Level:** The closing price on 2023-12-29 was $90.29. The 52-week high was $118.18 and the low was $78.73.
*   **Returns:** The stock returned +11.4% over the last quarter (from $81.05 on 2023-09-29) and +7.3% over the last year (from $84.17 on 2022-12-28).
*   **Context:** The recent quarterly price increase of +11.4% occurred alongside a quarter (Q4 2023) with a QoQ revenue decline of -4.9% and a net margin of 3.3%. The annual return of +7.3% is modest and coincides with volatile earnings and a high trailing P/E.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
*   **Upside:** The primary quantitative upside is the strengthening balance sheet, with growing equity and declining liabilities. A sustained reduction in debt could improve financial flexibility.
*   **Downside:** The primary quantitative downside is the extreme volatility and low quality of earnings, evidenced by a recent quarterly loss and inconsistent operating income. The high trailing P/E of 69.99 leaves no margin of safety for this earnings volatility. The complete absence of cash flow data is a significant analytical gap.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The quantitative risk/reward profile appears unfavorable. The stock is trading at a high valuation (P/E: 69.99) near the top of its recent range, which is not supported by the underlying earnings trajectory. Earnings are volatile and of low quality, with a recent quarterly loss and inconsistent margins. While the balance sheet is resilient, the lack of cash flow data and the disconnect between a high multiple and unstable fundamentals suggest the price has limited fundamental support at current levels. The recent price momentum (+11.4% quarterly) does not align with the latest quarterly fundamental performance.
DIS.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: The Walt Disney Company (DIS)**
**Decision Date:** 2024-01-01
**Analyst:** Qualitative Equity Research

**1. Business/Franchise Read from Supplied Narrative**
Based on the 2023-11-21 10-K filing, The Walt Disney Company is a diversified worldwide entertainment company operating through three segments: Entertainment, Sports, and Experiences. Its franchise is built on a portfolio of iconic intellectual property (Disney, Pixar, Marvel, Star Wars, National Geographic) and a vertically integrated model spanning content creation, linear television networks, direct-to-consumer (DTC) streaming services, and theme parks/resorts. The company employs approximately 225,000 people and emphasizes human capital development, diversity, and sustainability. The DTC segment, including Disney+, Hulu, and Disney+ Hotstar, is a central strategic focus, with ad-supported tier launches underway internationally.

**2. Dated Recent Developments (Pre-2024-01-01)**
*   **Streaming & Competition (2023-12-29):** A report indicates "complete and utter panic" among traditional entertainment companies, including Disney, facing massive streaming losses (~$5 billion cited for the group) in competition with Netflix. This is leading to pondered mergers and cost cuts among rivals.
*   **Strategic Outlook (2023-11-22, 2023-12-02):** Post-Q4 earnings (reported Nov. 2023), market commentary notes Disney stock rose due to streaming strength, subscriber additions, and an outlook for improved future costs and free cash flow. Separately, leadership (Iger, Pitaro) is researching the launch of an enhanced ESPN streaming service with advanced features.
*   **Corporate Actions (2023-11-21):** NBC Universal exercised its put right to require Disney to purchase its 33% stake in Hulu.
*   **Content & Advertising (2023-12-28):** A competitive development: Amazon's Prime Video will introduce ads on Jan. 29, 2024, with a pay-to-avoid option, highlighting an industry-wide shift towards ad-tier monetization.
*   **Legal & Regulatory Risks (Multiple dates, Dec. 2023):** Disney is engaged in multiple legal disputes in Florida, including a lawsuit alleging retaliation by Governor DeSantis's administration for political speech, and a separate lawsuit against the DeSantis-appointed district for failing to comply with public records requests. Disney is also defending a class-action lawsuit over background check policies and is suing over property taxes.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Intense Streaming Competition & Losses (2023-12-29):** The entertainment industry is characterized by severe competition and sustained financial losses in streaming, necessitating strategic pivots towards cost discipline, potential industry consolidation, and advertising revenue.
*   **Risk: Political/Legal Volatility in Florida (2023-12-12, 2023-12-17, 2023-12-27):** Ongoing litigation with Florida's government creates operational and reputational uncertainty, though Disney's 10-K and a Forbes article note Disney World's $40 billion economic impact on the state, providing a significant counterweight.
*   **Risk: Human Capital & Content Production (2023-11-21):** The 10-K notes that a significant number of employees, including writers, directors, and actors, are under collective bargaining agreements. Recent work stoppages are acknowledged as a risk that could impact the number of film and episodic titles produced in fiscal 2024.
*   **Offset: Strategic Cost Management & Streaming Profitability Path (2023-11-15, 2023-11-16):** Market analysis points to successful cost-cutting measures and bullish focus within Disney, with recent earnings showing streaming strength and a positive outlook for future costs and free cash flow.
*   **Offset: Durable IP and Integrated Model (2023-11-21):** The 10-K details a vast, century-old content library and a business model that monetizes IP across film, TV, DTC, and Experiences, providing structural resilience.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral with a Positive Bias.** Disney's franchise benefits from unparalleled intellectual property and an integrated entertainment ecosystem. Recent narrative (Nov. 2023) suggests a tangible inflection point in streaming profitability and cost control. However, the company faces material headwinds: an industry-wide streaming panic with sustained losses, complex legal entanglements in Florida, and content production risks. The strategic response—including potential Hulu integration, ESPN streaming innovation, and ad-tier expansion—positions the company to navigate these challenges, but near-term execution risk remains significant. The assessment balances strong foundational assets against acute operational and competitive pressures.

pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Disney's risk/reward is roughly balanced: a strengthening balance sheet and streaming profitability inflection offset a stretched trailing P/E of ~70x and highly volatile earnings. The stock is neither compelling enough to buy nor broken enough to sell at current levels.
key_factors: Trailing P/E of 69.99 sits near the top of its 2023 range (33.9–74.5), pricing in significant earnings recovery that has not yet materialized in stable quarterly results.
Q4 2023 net income of $694M (EPS $0.15) and a Q3 2023 net loss of -$153M demonstrate extreme earnings volatility, with TTM net margin of only 3.3%–3.8% and ROE of ~3.3%.
Balance sheet resilience: total equity grew for 8 consecutive quarters to $103.96B, debt-to-equity of 0.447, and total liabilities declining from $109B to $101.6B.

Streaming inflection narrative (Nov 2023 earnings showed subscriber strength and cost discipline) is credible but faces industry-wide competitive pressure, with Amazon Prime Video launching ads Jan 29, 2024 and the NBC Universal Hulu put option creating a ~$8.6B cash obligation.

INTC.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: INTC (as of 2024-01-01)**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
- **Revenue**: Latest Q3 2023 revenue was $14.16B, down -7.7% YoY from $15.34B in Q3 2022. TTM revenue (sum of last 4 quarters) is $52.86B. The quarterly trend shows recovery from a low of $11.71B in Q1 2023.
- **Earnings**: Q3 2023 net income was $310.00M (EPS $0.07), a sharp decline from Q2 2023's $1.47B (EPS $0.35). TTM net income is -$1.65B. YoY net income fell -69.6% from $1.02B in Q3 2022.
- **Margins**: Q3 2023 gross margin was 42.5% ($6.02B / $14.16B), improving from 35.9% in Q2 2023. Operating margin was effectively 0% (operating income -$8.00M). TTM operating margin is -6.8% (-$3.62B / $52.86B).
- **Cash-Flow**: No cash flow data is supplied for any period.
- **Balance Sheet**: Total assets grew to $188.84B in Q3 2023 from $168.41B in Q4 2021. Total equity was $105.69B. Total liabilities were $83.15B. Debt data is only available for Q4 2022 ($38.11B) and Q4 2021 ($38.10B).

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality**: TTM net income is negative (-$1.65B). The latest quarter's positive net income ($310M) contrasts with three of the prior four quarters being negative. The YoY EPS decline of -72.0% and TTM operating loss of -$3.62B indicate weak earnings durability.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Equity of $105.69B provides a buffer. The debt-to-equity ratio (using Q4 2022 debt of $38.11B and Q3 2023 equity of $105.69B) is approximately 0.36. However, the trajectory of liabilities is inconsistent in the data (some quarters show negative liabilities, likely a data error).

**3. Valuation versus Company's Own History**
- Latest valuation (2023-12-29): P/B = 2.011, P/S = 4.02, net margin = -3.1%, ROE = -1.6%.
- Historical range (2023): P/B ranged from 1.0 to 2.0; P/E diluted ranged from 8.3 to 17.1. The current P/B of 2.011 is at the top of its annual range. Net margin and ROE turned negative during 2023, from positive levels in early 2023 (e.g., net margin 19.1% on 2023-01-03).

**4. Price Level and Return Context vs. Fundamentals**
- **Price**: Closed at $50.25 on 2023-12-29. The 52-week range is $24.73 to $51.28.
- **Returns**: Quarterly return (from 2023-09-29) was +41.4%. Annual return (from 2022-12-28) was +96.8%.
- **Fundamental Context**: The price surge occurred alongside deteriorating fundamentals: TTM net income is negative, revenue is below year-ago levels, and margins compressed. The stock has more than doubled in a year while TTM earnings are negative.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
- **Upside**: The balance sheet remains large ($188.84B assets, $105.69B equity). A return to historical profitability levels (e.g., Q4 2021 net income of $4.62B) would significantly improve earnings. The recent Q3 2023 showed a gross margin rebound to 42.5%.
- **Downside**: TTM operating income is -$3.62B, and net income is -$1.65B. The stock trades at a P/B of 2.011, the high end of its range, despite negative ROE (-1.6%). The price has risen +96.8% annually while fundamentals have weakened, creating a potential disconnect.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The quantitative evidence shows a company with weakening fundamentals (negative TTM earnings, declining YoY revenue) but a sharply rising stock price. Valuation metrics (P/B at 2.011) are elevated relative to history and current profitability (ROE -1.6%). The balance sheet provides resilience, but the lack of cash flow data and persistent operating losses are concerns. The price has significantly outpaced the fundamental trajectory, suggesting elevated risk unless a sustained earnings recovery materializes.
INTC.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Analysis: Intel (INTC) as of 2024-01-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read from Narrative**
The supplied narrative characterizes Intel as a major technology franchise positioned at the intersection of CPU design and semiconductor manufacturing. Key attributes from the text include:
*   **Core Business:** Described as having a durable presence in the technology sector with innovation-driven revenue (2023-11-22). Its ecosystem integration is cited as a potential competitive advantage (2023-12-30).
*   **Strategic Direction:** The company is pursuing ambitious plans to become a leader in the foundry business and achieve technical/process node leadership (2023-12-21, 2023-12-29, 2023-11-21). It is actively targeting the AI computing market with new chips (2023-12-30).
*   **Competitive & Financial Position:** The narrative explicitly states Intel remains behind AMD by most measures and has not yet stopped its revenue decline (2023-12-21). However, its price-to-sales (P/S) ratio is noted as less than half of AMD's and near historical averages (2023-12-21).

**2. Recent Dated Developments (Pre-2024-01-01)**
*   **Legal/Security:** A class-action lawsuit was filed concerning the "Downfall" CPU vulnerability, alleging Intel knew about it since 2018. The lawsuit contends that the mitigation patches severely degrade performance, leaving CPUs "defective" or slowed down (2023-11-13, 2023-12-08). Separately, the Federal Circuit issued a decision in the VLSI v. Intel patent case regarding damages apportionment (2023-12-14).
*   **Market Sentiment & Positioning:** Multiple market commentaries (2023-12-30) framed Intel as a potential outperformer and "dark horse" in AI for 2024, citing its ecosystem edge and new chip opportunities. One analysis noted Q4 guidance pointed toward a potential earnings surge (2023-11-26). Intel's stock price closed at $50.39 on 2023-12-28, reflecting a -0.73% change on that day (2023-12-28).

**3. Material Qualitative Risks & Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    1.  **Legal & Reputational Exposure:** The Downfall vulnerability lawsuits present direct financial risk and potential reputational damage, with allegations of delayed action exacerbating the issue (2023-11-13, 2023-12-08).
    2.  **Execution Risk:** The ambitious plans to lead in foundry and process technology face strong competition, primarily from AMD and non-x86 manufacturers (2023-11-21). The narrative notes these plans are not yet realized (2023-12-21).
    3.  **Competitive Erosion:** Persistent acknowledgment that Intel lags key competitors on several measures (2023-12-21).
*   **Offsets:**
    1.  **Valuation Narrative:** The text presents a relative valuation argument, noting Intel's lower P/S multiple compared to AMD (2023-12-21).
    2.  **Catalyst Narrative:** There is a recurring speculative narrative that Intel is well-positioned to capitalize on the AI boom through ecosystem integration and new products, potentially driving a stock price re-rating (2023-12-30, 2023-12-21).

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral.** The qualitative picture as of early 2024 is mixed. The franchise is under tangible pressure from significant legal risks (Downfall, VLSI) and acknowledged competitive gaps. This is balanced by a forward-looking narrative centered on a potential AI-driven recovery and an attractive relative valuation. The assessment is neutral because the bullish catalysts (AI positioning) remain largely prospective, while the bearish risks (lawsuits, execution) are present in the current evidence. The stock's price action on 2023-12-28, underperforming a broader market uptick, may reflect market skepticism towards the near-term catalyst narrative.

- : The qualitative report quotes a source stating 'Intel's financial performance has been a testament to its enduring presence... consistent revenue growth over the years' — this is directly contradicted by the raw quantitative evidence showing revenue declining from $20.53B (Q4 2021) to $14.16B (Q3 2023) and TTM revenue well below prior peaks. The report does not flag this inconsistency.
- : The qualitative report states 'Q4 guidance points toward a potential earnings surge' without noting that no forward guidance or Q4 actuals appear in the supplied quantitative packs. This is an unverified forward-looking claim presented as fact.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: INTC's stock has surged +96.8% over the past year while fundamentals remain deeply impaired—TTM net income of -$1.65B, negative operating margins, and revenue still below year-ago levels. The stock now trades at the top of its historical P/B range (2.011) despite negative ROE (-1.6%), creating a valuation that prices in substantial recovery that has not yet materialized in the financials. The risk/reward is roughly balanced: further narrative-driven upside is possible on AI and foundry catalysts, but the stock is priced for execution that carries significant uncertainty.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: INTC's stock has surged +96.8% over the past year while fundamentals remain deeply impaired—TTM net income of -$1.65B, negative operating margins, and revenue still below year-ago levels. The stock now trades at the top of its historical P/B range (2.011) despite negative ROE (-1.6%), creating a valuation that prices in substantial recovery that has not yet materialized in the financials. The risk/reward is roughly balanced: further narrative-driven upside is possible on AI and foundry catalysts, but the stock is priced for execution that carries significant uncertainty.
key_factors: TTM net income of -$1.65B with TTM operating margin of -6.8% (-$3.62B / $52.86B revenue), reflecting persistent fundamental deterioration despite the stock's +96.8% annual price surge
P/B of 2.011 sits at the top of the 1-year range (1.0-2.0) while ROE is -1.6%, indicating the market has priced in a recovery that negative earnings have not yet validated
Q3 2023 gross margin rebounded to 42.5% from 35.9% in Q2 2023, and quarterly revenue recovered to $14.16B from a Q1 2023 low of $11.71B, suggesting the worst of the revenue trough may be behind

Legal and execution risks remain material: Downfall vulnerability class-action lawsuit (filed 2023-11-13) and acknowledged competitive gaps versus AMD, while foundry and AI positioning remain largely prospective narratives

  filing_date=2023-02-09 section=business period_end=2022-12-31
Item 1. BUSINESS
Amgen Inc. (including its subsidiaries, referred to as "Amgen," "the Company," "we," "our" or "us") is a biotechnology company committed to unlocking the potential of biology for patients suffering from serious illnesses by discovering, developing, manufacturing and delivering innovative human therapeutics. This approach begins by using tools like advanced human genetics to unravel the complexities of disease and understand the fundamentals of human biology.
Amgen focuses on areas of high unmet medical need and leverages its expertise to strive for solutions that improve health outcomes and dramatically improve people's lives. A biotechnology pioneer, Amgen has grown to be one of the world's leading independent biotechnology companies, has reached millions of patients around the world and is developing a pipeline of medicines with breakaway potential.
Amgen was incorporated in California in 1980 and became a Delaware corporation in 1987. We have a presence in approximately 100 countries worldwide. Amgen operates in one business segment: human therapeutics.
Significant Developments
Following is a summary of significant developments affecting our business that have occurred and that we have reported since the filing of our Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2021.
Acquisitions
Proposed acquisition of Horizon Therapeutics plc
- On December 12, 2022, we announced that we entered into a transaction agreement under which Amgen will acquire all shares of Horizon for $116.50 per share in cash for 
…[truncated 20464 chars]…
e New Jersey District Court. We disagree with the court's holding and we are in the process of appealing this judgment. See Part IV—Note 19, Contingencies and commitments, to the Consolidated Financial Statements, Amgen Inc. v. Sandoz Inc., et al.
(3)A patent with this subject matter may be entitled to patent term extension in the United States.
7
Competition
We operate in a highly competitive environment. A number of our marketed products are indicated for disease areas in which other products or treatments are currently available or are being pursued by our competitors through R&D activities. Additionally, some competitor-marketed products target the same genetic pathways as our recently launched marketed products or are currently in development. This competition could impact the pricing and market share of our products. We continue to pursue ways of increasing the value of our medicines through innovations during their life cycles, which can include expanding the disease areas for which our products are indicated and finding new methods to make the delivery of our medicines easier and less costly. Such activities can offer important opportunities for differentiation. For example, we market the Neulasta Onpro kit, which provides physicians the opportunity to initiate administration of the recommended dose of Neulasta on the same day as chemotherapy, with drug delivery at home the day after chemotherapy, thereby saving the patient a trip back to the doctor. We plan to continue pursuing innovation efforts to strengthen our competitive position. Such position may be based on, among other things, safety, efficacy, reliability, availability, patient convenience, delivery devices, price, reimbursement, access to and timing of market entry and patent position and expiration.
Certain of the existing patents on our principal products have expired, and we face new and increasing competition, including from biosimilars and generics. A biosimilar is another version of a biological product for which marketing approval is sought or has been obtained based on a demonstration that it is "highly similar" to the original reference product. We have experienced adverse effects from biosimilar competition on our originator product sales. Companies have launched biosimilar versions of EPOGEN, NEUPOGEN and Neulasta and have approved biosimilars for ENBREL. Once multiple biosimilar versions of one of our originator products have launched, competition has intensified rapidly, resulting in greater net price declines for both reference and biosimilar products and a greater effect on product sales. See also Government Regulation—Regulation in the United States—Approval of Biosimilars. Although competitor biosimilars compete on price, we believe many patients, providers and payers will continue to place high value on the reputation, supply reliability and safety of our products. As additional biosimilar competitors come to market, we will continue to leverage our global experience to distinguish against both branded and biosimilar competition.
We also have our own biosimilar products both in the United States and outside of U.S. markets that are competing against branded and biosimilar versions of our competitors' products. In 2019, Amgen launched MVASI, a biosimilar to Avastin, and KANJINTI, a biosimilar to Herceptin; and in 2018, Amgen launched AMGEVITA, a biosimilar to HUMIRA in markets outside the United States. We have also received FDA approval of AMJEVITA, a biosimilar to HUMIRA for the U.S. market, which launched in January 2023. In 2020, we launched AVSOLA, a biosimilar to Remicade; and in 2021 we launched RIABNI, a biosimilar to Rituxan. We expect additional biosimilar competition against both our branded and biosimilar products in the future across markets.
Although most of our products are biologics, some are small molecule products. Because the FDA approval process permits generic manufacturers to rely on the safety and efficacy data of the innovator product rather than having to conduct their own costly and time-consuming clinical trials, generic manufacturers can often develop and market their competing versions of our small molecule products at much lower prices. For example, following loss of exclusivity of patents directed to cinacalcet, the active ingredient in our small molecule calcimimetic Sensipar, we lost a significant share of the market and corresponding revenues in a very short period of time. See Part IV—Note 19, Contingencies and commitments, to the Consolidated Financial Statements.
The introduction of new products, the development of new processes or technologies by competitors or the emergence of new information about existing products may result in (i) increased competition for our marketed products, even for those protected by patents and/or (ii) reductions in the prices we receive from selling our products. In addition, the development of new treatment options or standards of care may reduce the use of our products or may limit the utility and application of ongoing clinical trials of our product candidates. (As used in this document, the term clinical trials may include prospective clinical trials, observational studies, registries and other studies.) See Item 1A. Risk Factors—Our products face substantial competition and our product candidates are also likely to face substantial competition and Item 1A. Risk Factors—We currently face competition from biosimilars and generics and expect to face increasing competition from biosimilars and generics in the future.
8
